session_note: S73 (user GO -- full autonomous batch, highest quality + full landing audit before playtest). P1 = #1 STABLE LOBBY SEATS (non-compacting), Standard PDR, 3-way Council ADOPT-WITH-CHANGES. Then a full Rule-22 + landing audit of all S71/S72 work (bomb/hunter/potato).
council_battle_ledger: S73 P1 Standard 3-way Council (Claude+Grok-4.20-reasoning+Gemini-2.5-pro); both ADOPT-WITH-CHANGES. ADOPTED: stateful sequence-fold test (reconcile is PURE -> fold over join/join/leave-middle/back-fill/Begin; the real regression surface) + seat-uniqueness/host-0/seats-in-[1,MAX-1] invariant assertion (Grok G4) + no-hole stable==dense canary. PARTIAL: Grok G1 NEW-peer ordering -- REJECT the canonical peerId-sort (arrival-order = peerSet insertion order is the intuitive contract; lobby seating is NOT replayed by save.replay), adopt explicit doc-it. REJECT: Gemini Ge4 PROTOCOL_VERSION bump -- premise factual error (the roster is a COMPACT array of OCCUPIED seats with a non-contiguous seat FIELD, NOT a sparse/null array; isValidRoster protocol.ts:329 has no contiguity check; the client renders the RECEIVED roster via lobbyView bySeat.get(i) lobbyStateMachine.ts:329, never a local positional recompute; Begin START_GAME_SIGNAL stays dense) -> no-bump per the S70 cosmetic-presence precedent + friends-only single-deploy. CARRY-FORWARD #4: TOCTOU late-leave at Begin (PRE-EXISTING, not worsened -- clients seat from msg.roster not local peerIds), host-migration must init lobbySeats fresh, Option-2 sparse-in-game (spawn-by-ordinal). DECISIONS: Q1 1b single-source-of-truth (1a diverges on back-fill -- both reviewers concede); Q2 Option 1 dense Begin (Option 2 deferred); Q3 lowest-free fill; Q4 pure fold (no Pixi extraction).
prime_audit: S73 PRIME-AUDIT: consensus-masking-disagreement SURFACED -- Gemini said BUMP, I refute NO-BUMP with 3 verified code facts (isValidRoster no-contiguity protocol.ts:329; client bySeat.get(i) renders RECEIVED roster lobbyStateMachine.ts:329; Begin stays dense). No-bump CONFIRMED. Runtime-verifiability: pure fns runtime-covered by vitest; host wiring by real-browser Playwright lobby/presence specs (the boot-then-smoke). Mtime N/A (no leak/regression-landing claim). Materially > R1 (adds stateful sequence fold + invariant + no-hole canary). Confidence HIGH.

priorities.label: S73 P1 (Standard) -- STABLE (non-compacting) LOBBY SEATS. When a player leaves the lobby, survivors KEEP their seat+color; the leaver seat shows empty; a new joiner fills the LOWEST empty seat. Root cause: seat = index in transport.peerIds() (a compacting Set). Fix (Option 1b, single source of truth): host holds session.lobbySeats Map<peerId,seat>; pure reconcileLobbySeats(prev,peerIds) keeps/drops/lowest-free-assigns; pure buildLobbyRoster(map,selfId) -> STABLE projection (holes ok) for LOBBY_PRESENCE; pure buildMatchRoster(map,selfId) -> DENSE contiguous 0..N-1 projection for START_GAME_SIGNAL + hostSeats (radialSpawnPos needs dense seats). PROTOCOL_VERSION stays 5 (no wire shape change). Render layer UNCHANGED (lobbyView bySeat.get(i) already handles holes). Files: net/lobbyRoster(+test), net/hostHandlers, net/session. GATE: tsc + knip0 + vitest + save.replay + build<=550KB + Playwright gating lane. CHECK Triumvirate + Rule22.
priorities.check_method: S73 P1 (stable non-compacting lobby seats) VERIFIED + SHIPPED c9cf9be. ROOT CAUSE: lobby seat = index in transport.peerIds() (a compacting JS Set) -> a mid-roster leave shifted every later peer down a seat + changed their colour. FIX (Council Option 1b, single source of truth): host holds session.lobbySeats Map<peerId,seat> (seats 1..MAX-1; host=0), reconciled on each onPeerChange via PURE reconcileLobbySeats (survivors KEEP their seat; departed peer frees a HOLE; new peer takes the LOWEST free seat; over-cap unseated), projected two ways -- buildLobbyRoster (STABLE, holes ok) -> LOBBY_PRESENCE + host rack; buildMatchRoster (DENSE contiguous 0..N-1, re-densified ascending-seat order) -> START_GAME_SIGNAL + hostSeats freeze (radialSpawnPos REQUIRES contiguous seats else avatars overlap: seat 3/total 3 == seat 0). NO PROTOCOL_VERSION bump (stays v5; wire shape unchanged -- a holed LOBBY_PRESENCE is a COMPACT array of occupied seats with a non-contiguous seat FIELD, valid under isValidRoster length<=MAX; client renders the RECEIVED roster via lobbyView bySeat.get(i), holes -> empty cells). Render layer (lobbyStateMachine/seatRack/lobbyScreen/main.ts digest) UNTOUCHED. teardownNet + a defensive host-start reset both clear lobbySeats. GATES: tsc 0, knip 0, vitest 1108 (+12 net over 1096: reconcile join-order/leave-frees-hole/back-fill-lowest/multi-hole-multi-join/cap-unseated/idempotent/invariant-across-churn, buildLobbyRoster holes-ordered, buildMatchRoster dense+colour+fills-MAX, no-hole stable==dense canary, fold-over-event-sequence), save.replay byte-equivalence GREEN (lobby seating is real-time presence, NOT replayed), vite build 529.66KB (<550 charter, +0.62KB), Playwright gating lane 27 pass + 1 pre-existing skip (S70 presence drop-on-leave, S62 3-player FFA seat/colour, S63 6-player, v2/v6 protocol-mismatch ALL green -> no-bump validated, zero regression). CHECK TRIUMVIRATE: RALPH:PATROL CLEAN (all gates green = backpressure sweep). GROK-ANALYST ADOPT (verified no off-by-one in lowest-free scan, sort determinism, leave+rejoin keeps seat, dup-PID idempotence, colour-OOB safe, hostSeats==START_GAME agreement by construction, no prev-mutation/aliasing). GEMINI-AUDITOR FIX-THEN-SHIP -> triaged against the ACTUAL code: (c) "Play Again reuses stale lobbySeats" = FALSE-POSITIVE bug (FSM has NO POSTGAME->LOBBY path; every re-Host routes POSTGAME->TITLE->teardownNet which clears) but ADOPTED the cheap defensive host-start clear (belt-and-suspenders, mirrors applyStartGame hazard-clears); (e) "sort peerIds for determinism" REJECTED (lobby seating is ARRIVAL-ordered = the intended first-to-join-lower-seat contract, NOT part of save.replay; a peerId-sort would make seats arbitrary + worse UX) -- same call as PLAN-phase Grok G1; (f) multi-hole/multi-join test gap ADOPTED. NET SHIP: 1 ADOPT + 1 FIX-THEN-SHIP whose blocker was a verified false-positive; 2 genuine improvements adopted (defensive clear + test), 1 reasoned reject. PRIME-AUDIT delta: SURFACED + REFUTED Gemini's must-bump disagreement with 3 verified code facts (isValidRoster no-contiguity protocol.ts:329; client bySeat.get(i) renders received roster lobbyStateMachine.ts:329; Begin stays dense). Rule22 runtime audit CLEAN: pure TS net/lobby logic + tests; no env-VAR placeholders / Docker / CLI flags / restart-survival paths; all imports resolve (tsc 0); the real-browser Playwright lobby/presence specs ARE the boot-then-smoke. Post-push CI (deploy + e2e) triggered on c9cf9be -> confirm green in the landing audit. checkpoint c9cf9be.

priorities.label: S73 P2 (Micro; Scope Amendment, surfaced by the landing audit) -- BOMB WIN-edge TEARDOWN PARITY. WIN_TRIGGER clears world.hunters (teardownHunters) + world.potatoes (teardownPotatoes) but NOT world.bombs (asymmetry: a bomb live at WIN lingers ~2s on the win screen; cosmetic, cannot be grabbed/leak). Add teardownBombs() to bombLifecycle.ts (mirror the other two) + call it in the WIN_TRIGGER case of world.ts + a bombLifecycle.test WIN-clears-bombs case. Completes the all-three-hazards-teardown-on-every-PLAYING-exit invariant. Files: state/bombLifecycle(+test), state/world. GATE: tsc + knip0 + vitest + build + Playwright. CHECK Micro (RALPH + GROK-ANALYST).
priorities.council_waiver_reason: Micro-tier scope-amendment (3 LOC teardown helper exactly mirroring the already-reviewed teardownHunters/teardownPotatoes + 1 test); user pre-approved the full batch. CHECK = RALPH:PATROL + GROK-ANALYST (Micro), not full Triumvirate.
priorities.check_method: S73 P2 (bomb WIN-edge teardown parity, Micro scope-amendment) VERIFIED + SHIPPED 90104b9. SURFACED BY the S73 landing audit (Rule 22): WIN_TRIGGER cleared world.hunters (teardownHunters) + world.potatoes (teardownPotatoes) but NOT world.bombs -> a bomb live at the win moment lingered ~2s on the win screen (cosmetic: cannot be grabbed once out of PLAYING, and START_GAME + RETURN_TO_TITLE already clear bombs before the next match). FIX: new teardownBombs(world) in bombLifecycle.ts (world.bombs.clear() + nextBombId=0; mirrors the already-shipped teardownHunters/teardownPotatoes; the bomb carries NO player-side state so there is no benchedUntilTick/carriedPotatoId analog) + called in the WIN_TRIGGER case of world.ts alongside the other two -> completes the all-three-hazards-teardown-on-every-PLAYING-exit invariant S72 P4 was building toward. GATES: tsc 0, knip 0, vitest 1110 (+2: teardownBombs clears Map+counter; dispatched WIN_TRIGGER clears a live bomb + sets gameState WIN), vite build 529.74KB (<550, +0.08KB), Playwright gating 27 pass + 1 pre-existing skip (Sym I WIN_TRIGGER+ENDGAME path GREEN -> the win-flow change verified no-regression in a real browser). CHECK Micro: RALPH:PATROL CLEAN (all gates green); GROK-ANALYST ADOPT. Self-verified the snapshot/replay concern (not just the bare ADOPT): post-WIN host snapshot carries empty bombs[], client rebuilds bombs each apply (clears in lockstep -> no desync), save.replay world has no bombs (vitest green incl. save.replay). Rule22 runtime audit CLEAN: pure TS reducer state-clear + tests; no env/Docker/CLI/restart paths; the real-browser Sym I e2e IS the boot-then-smoke for the WIN edge. checkpoint 90104b9.

reflexion_entries_to_archive: P1 #stable-lobby-seats #council-standard #single-source-of-truth #no-bump #stateful-pure-fold: shipped non-compacting lobby seats (c9cf9be) -- the host holds a persistent peerId->seat map reconciled on each peer join/leave (survivors keep their seat, a departed peer leaves a HOLE, a new joiner fills the LOWEST free seat), projected STABLE for the lobby preview (holes ok) and DENSE-compacted for the authoritative Begin roster (radialSpawnPos needs contiguous seats). DEFINING MOVES: (1) the SCOPE-DEFINING research finding -- radialSpawnPos(seat,total)=angle pi+(seat/total)*2pi OVERLAPS avatars on a holed roster (seat 3 with total 3 == seat 0 angle), so the GAME requires contiguous seats -> the lobby can be stable (holed) but Begin MUST compact, which bounded the whole PDR to net/lobby (zero determinism-core change) and deferred sparse-in-game-seats to #4. (2) caught a CORRECTNESS LANDMINE in design: "stable lobby + positional Begin" (Option 1a) DIVERGES -- a back-filled hole is assigned differently than peerSet insertion order -> a lobby<->game colour swap; the fix is ONE seat-map projected two ways (Option 1b), preserving S70's DRY preview==Begin unification. (3) NO PROTOCOL_VERSION bump -- Gemini (PLAN + CHECK) insisted on a bump on a FACTUAL MISREAD ("sparse array with nulls"); refuted with 3 verified code facts (isValidRoster no-contiguity, client renders received roster via bySeat.get(i), Begin stays dense) -- the roster is a COMPACT array whose seat FIELD is non-contiguous, not a sparse/null array. (4) the PURE reconcile let the stateful host loop be tested by FOLDING reconcile over a join/leave event sequence -- no Pixi extraction needed (resolved the Council test-strategy challenge elegantly). (5) CHECK discipline held: Gemini FIX-THEN-SHIP blocker (stale-lobbySeats) verified false-positive against the FSM, adopted the cheap defensive clear anyway, rejected the determinism-sort WITH reasoning. LESSON: when a "stable" UX request meets a determinism-critical core, find the SEAM (lobby-stable + dense-at-the-boundary) that fixes the UX without touching the core; and a reviewer's "you must bump the protocol" is a hypothesis to verify against the actual wire-validator + client render path, not an order.

P2 #audit-surfaced #hazard-teardown-parity #micro-amendment #invariant-completion: shipped the bomb WIN-edge teardown (90104b9) -- a Micro scope-amendment the S73 LANDING AUDIT surfaced. WIN_TRIGGER tore down hunters + potatoes but not bombs, so a bomb live at the win moment lingered ~2s on the win screen. Added teardownBombs() mirroring teardownHunters/teardownPotatoes + called it on the PLAYING->WIN edge. DEFINING MOVE: this is the AUDIT DOING ITS JOB -- a parallel deep-trace subagent (one per hazard: bomb/hunter/potato) caught an asymmetry that prior per-feature CHECKs missed, because each hazard was reviewed in isolation; only a CROSS-hazard parity sweep (does EACH exit edge clear ALL THREE?) surfaces it. The S72 P4 "all three hazards start-of-match invariant" completed START_GAME + RETURN_TO_TITLE for the bomb, but the WIN-edge teardown was added per-hazard (teardownHunters S72P2, teardownPotatoes S72P3) and the bomb -- shipped in S71 BEFORE those helpers existed -- was never retrofitted onto the WIN edge. LESSON: invariants built up feature-by-feature ("all N hazards teardown everywhere") need a periodic CROSS-CUTTING matrix audit (each entity x each exit edge), because incremental per-feature CHECKs verify the NEW feature's coverage of all edges but not the OLDER features' coverage of the NEW edge. Scope-amendment discipline held: a Micro PDR amendment was written + gated (P2 entry, council_waived user-path) BEFORE the 3-LOC edit -- not an ad-hoc urgency fix.

fork_decisions.fork_1b_vs_1a: 1b single-source-of-truth stable seat-map (1a positional-Begin diverges on back-fill)
fork_decisions.fork_option1_vs_2: Option 1 (stable lobby preview + DENSE Begin roster); Option 2 sparse-in-game deferred to #4
fork_decisions.fork_hole_fill: lowest-free seat for new joiners (incumbents never move; rack stays compact)
fork_decisions.fork_protocol_bump: NO bump (stays v5) -- wire shape unchanged; holed LOBBY_PRESENCE is a valid <=MAX array; client renders received roster

carry_forward: === RESOLVED S72 === P2 PAC-MAN HUNTER (97e4616): leader-reaches-75% spawns ONCE a host-authoritative hunter chasing the avatar via deterministic capped-momentum pursuit (juke-able); catch -> 30s bench (avatar hidden + input locked) + DROP_SPARK; 30s escape -> despawn. SEPARATE world.hunters Map; CRITICAL target-disconnect crash-guard; solo avatarPos fix; no protocol bump; additive snapshot. P3 POTATO BOMB (76af09a): carryable from-SPAWN-fuse (Fork E user) radial-AoE weapon; SEPARATE world.potatoes Map + separate potatoRng (spark+bomb byte-identical); carry-1 exclusive w/ spark (both directions); deterministic position-based AoE (squared-dist + sorted PrimitiveId/BondId; owner-agnostic; no chain reaction); carrier-disconnect force-detonate (sim-loop poll); NO protocol bump (Council batch-bump). P4 (309024c): detonation BOOM SFX (covers bomb+potato) + defensive applyStartGame bomb-clear. Teardown invariant for all 3 hazards on WIN/RETURN_TO_TITLE/START_GAME. === LOGGED CHECK CARRY-FORWARDS (S72, not dropped) === (a) NO-BUMP cosmetic mixed-version gap: a stale-v5 (P2-build) client joining a P3-build host sees prims vanish from a potato AoE without rendering the potato -- accepted friends-only trade-off (both CHECK reviewers validated no-bump); if a future feature needs cross-version safety, revisit the S52 new-client-intent->bump precedent. (b) Centralized PLAYING-exit teardown (Gemini P3): the current per-trigger (WIN_TRIGGER) + RETURN_TO_TITLE + START_GAME-invariant fully covers the actual FSM (no DRAW state), but a single PLAYING-exit teardown hook would be cleaner if the FSM grows. (c) spawner rng/bombRng/potatoRng not serialized -> save/load resets spawn cadence (PRE-EXISTING spark-spawner posture; folds into netcode/save-determinism backlog). (d) hunter disconnect-fade (PDR locks immediate despawn; optional polish). (e) Fork E potato fuse from-SPAWN is one-line-flippable to from-placement (makePotato + applyPlacePotato) if playtest prefers Council's. === CONFIRM AT NEXT BOOT (Rule 22) === P3 (76af09a) + P4 (309024c) post-push CI E2E were in-flight at handoff (local Playwright 27/27 green); confirm both green. === PRE-EXISTING BACKLOG (prioritized for next session; NOT started S72 -- ORANGE budget + each is multi-session) === 1. STABLE LOBBY SEATS (non-compacting) -- likely the most tractable + highest user-value next; bounded scope (lobby/seating). 2. CONTROL-MESSAGE SENDER-AUTH -- security hardening; bounded, raises the v1 threat model past friends-only. 3. EYES fog fuzzy-edge + CVD shape-icons -- render/accessibility feature; self-contained. 4. LIVE-PLAY NETCODE INFRA (host-migration / reconnect / 6p delta) -- LARGEST (genuinely multi-session); also the home for the spawner-rng-serialization + the no-bump cross-version work above. Recommend: tackle #1 or #3 as a single-priority Standard PDR next session; #4 needs its own multi-session plan. === OTHER (pre-existing) === main.ts Pixi/DOM shell hypertrophy (de-hypertrophy candidate).
